text:
BySamindra Kunti,

Former Contributor.

Mallory Swanson’s first-half brace led the U.S. to a convincing 3-0 win against Zambia on its Olympic debut, marking new manager Emma Hayes’s first victory at a major finals.

Without Alex Morgan, left out, and Megan Rapinoe, retired, the U.S. steamrollered its opponent, reduced to ten players after the half-hour mark, with slick attacking play and scored at will in the first half to dispel lingering doubts after an unconvincing goalless draw against Costa Rica in its final warm-up match.

In France, the U.S. is in search of a fifth gold medal and this time the American offense did not sputter even if both captain Lindsey Horan, tapping wide from a few yards out, and Swanson, clipping the bar, missed major opportunities in the opening exchanges. Sophia Smith rattled the woodwork as well.

The attacking trio of Swanson, Smith, and Trinity Rodman provided pace and panache, bamboozling the Zambian defense, and the first stanza was something of a shooting gallery for Hayes’s team. It was only a matter of time before the U.S. would score, and, connecting with a Horan pass, Rodman found the opener in the 17th minute with a slick finish.

The Copper Queens massed behind the ball and lurked on the counter, but seldom found an attacking outlet. That strategy of bunkering and counterattacking backfired. The U.S. breezed past Zambia with Swanson, earning her 95th cap, getting a brace midway through the first half.Her two strikes - 66 seconds apart - were the fastest two goals by a single player in a major tournament for the U.S. team.

Horan, by then, had two assists to her name. To compound Zambia’s misery, defender Pauline Zulu got sent off in the 33rd minute following an on-field review that upgraded her yellow card after contact with Smith.

In a largely academic second half, the U.S. retained possession and controlled the match without ever hitting the heights of its impressive first half. That suited the Copper Queens, who had gone into damage-control mode. At the Olympic Games in Tokyo, Zambia conceded ten times in its opener against the Netherlands. There was no danger of such a mammoth score as second-half attempts by Rodman and Crystal Dunn, from close range, lacked precision.

Ultimately, the U.S. matched expectations and did what it had to against a modest opponent, even if the side dropped off considerably after the break. With 2016 Olympic champion Germany and Australia to come in Group B, the U.S. will face tougher opponents, but its Olympic campaign is up and running in the best way possible.
title: USWNT Kickstarts Olympic Campaign With 3-0 Victory Against Zambia
author: Samindra Kunti
description: Mallory Swanson, with a brace, and Trinity Rodman were on target for the Americans.
